title: zea mays var. hirta
intro: Zea mays var. hirta, also known as hair corn or hairy corn, is a subspecies of the domesticated corn plant (Zea mays) characterized by its hairy or fuzzy appearance. Traditionally, Zea mays var. hirta has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis, coughs, and colds due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's health benefits also include its potential to lower blood sugar levels and reduce inflammation in the body. Zea mays var. hirta can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
